questions.question: This cube has a side length of 4 units. What is its total surface area?

payload.setup: const geo = new THREE.BoxGeometry(2, 2, 2); const mat = new THREE.MeshStandardMaterial({color: 0x00ffcc, wireframe: true}); window.mesh = new THREE.Mesh(geo, mat); scene.add(window.mesh); camera.position.z = 4;

questions.question: How many boxes are currently being affected by gravity in this simulation?

payload.setup: for(let i=0; i<6; i++) { const box = Matter.Bodies.rectangle(100 + (i*40), 0, 30, 30, { restitution: 0.5 }); Matter.World.add(world, box); }

questions.question: What is the name of the 3D shape rotating on your screen?

payload.setup: const geo = new THREE.TorusGeometry(1.5, 0.5, 16, 100); const mat = new THREE.MeshPhongMaterial({color: 0xff007a, shininess: 100}); window.donut = new THREE.Mesh(geo, mat); scene.add(window.donut);

questions.question: A store offers a 25% discount on a $120 jacket. What is the final price?
The ball in this simulation has 'high restitution' (bounciness). If it falls from 300px, will it eventually stop bouncing?
options: No, it is a perfect vacuum

payload.setup: const ball = Matter.Bodies.circle(200, 50, 25, { restitution: 0.9, frictionAir: 0.01 }); Matter.World.add(world, ball);

questions.question: This plane is oscillating. If its center is at y=0, and it moves up to y=2, what is the 'amplitude' of the wave?

payload.setup: const geo = new THREE.PlaneGeometry(3, 3); const mat = new THREE.MeshBasicMaterial({color: 0x6366f1, side: THREE.DoubleSide}); window.p = new THREE.Mesh(geo, mat); scene.add(window.p);

questions.question: What is the area of a triangle with a base of 10cm and a height of 8cm?
Observe the 3D Knot. How many loops does a 'Trefoil Knot' traditionally have?

payload.setup: const geo = new THREE.TorusKnotGeometry(1, 0.4, 100, 16); const mat = new THREE.MeshNormalMaterial(); window.knot = new THREE.Mesh(geo, mat); scene.add(window.knot);
